topic: A student receives a much lower grade than expected
raw_output:
[story 1]

The red ink on the paper seemed to mock me. A C- minus. After all those late nights, the sheer volume of research, the meticulous outlining—it all collapsed into this dismal number. I slammed my textbook shut, the sound echoing sharply in the quiet library aisle. My jaw tightened, and I could feel a hot flush creeping up my neck. "Are you serious?" I muttered to the empty air, shoving the graded paper into my backpack with unnecessary force. I needed to see the actual feedback, the specifics, right now. I pulled out my phone, fingers flying across the screen, already formulating questions, already planning the next move. This wasn't acceptable.

[story 2]

I stared at the transcript, my eyes darting back and forth across the low score. It felt like a physical weight pressing down on my chest, making every breath shallow. I tapped my pen rapidly against the desk, a nervous rhythm building in my hands. *This can't be right.* I reread the rubric, searching for some hidden error, a misinterpretation. My shoulders tensed, pulling up towards my ears. I stood up abruptly, pacing the small space between the shelves, the floorboards groaning under my hurried steps. I needed an explanation, a justification for this outcome, and the silence of the room was amplifying the churning frustration inside me.

[story 3]

The notification popped up on my laptop screen, the grade stark and undeniable. A significant drop from what I had meticulously projected. I let out a slow, ragged exhale, the air feeling suddenly too thick to draw in. I leaned back in my chair, crossing my arms tightly over my chest, my gaze fixed on the ceiling as if the answer were written there in permanent marker. "Unbelievable," I whispered, the word tasting bitter. I began to furiously scroll through the online portal, clicking links and refreshing pages, my movements jerky and uncoordinated. Every second that passed felt like an eternity dragging on, and I just wanted this whole confusing situation to resolve itself immediately.
